text:
back/.devcontainer
```json back/.devcontainer/devcontainer.json
// For format details, see https://aka.ms/devcontainer.json. For config options, see the README at:
// https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-dev-containers/tree/v0.177.0/containers/docker-existing-docker-compose
// If you want to run as a non-root user in the container, see .devcontainer/docker-compose.yml.
{
	"name": "Existing Docker Compose (Extend)",

	// Update the 'dockerComposeFile' list if you have more compose files or use different names.
	// The .devcontainer/docker-compose.yml file contains any overrides you need/want to make.
	"dockerComposeFile": [
		"../docker-compose.yml",
		"docker-compose.yml"
	],

	// The 'service' property is the name of the service for the container that VS Code should
	// use. Update this value and .devcontainer/docker-compose.yml to the real service name.
	"service": "api",

	// The optional 'workspaceFolder' property is the path VS Code should open by default when
	// connected. This is typically a file mount in .devcontainer/docker-compose.yml
	"workspaceFolder": "/app",

	// Set *default* container specific settings.json values on container create.
	"settings": {
		"terminal.integrated.shell.linux": "/bin/bash",
	},

	// Add the IDs of extensions you want installed when the container is created.
	"extensions": [
		"ms-python.python",
		"njpwerner.autodocstring",
		"ms-python.black-formatter",
		"GitHub.copilot"
	]

	// Use 'forwardPorts' to make a list of ports inside the container available locally.
	// "forwardPorts": [],

	// Uncomment the next line if you want start specific services in your Docker Compose config.
	// "runServices": [],

	// Uncomment the next line if you want to keep your containers running after VS Code shuts down.
	// "shutdownAction": "none",

	// Uncomment the next line to run commands after the container is created - for example installing curl.
	// "postCreateCommand": "apt-get update && apt-get install -y curl",

	// Uncomment to connect as a non-root user if you've added one. See https://aka.ms/vscode-remote/containers/non-root.
	// "remoteUser": "vscode"
}
```

```yml back/.devcontainer/docker-compose.yml
version: '3'
services:
  # Update this to the name of the service you want to work with in your docker-compose.yml file
  api:
    # If you want add a non-root user to your Dockerfile, you can use the "remoteUser"
    # property in devcontainer.json to cause VS Code its sub-processes (terminals, tasks,
    # debugging) to execute as the user. Uncomment the next line if you want the entire
    # container to run as this user instead. Note that, on Linux, you may need to
    # ensure the UID and GID of the container user you create matches your local user.
    # See https://aka.ms/vscode-remote/containers/non-root for details.
    #
    # user: vscode

    # Uncomment if you want to override the service's Dockerfile to one in the .devcontainer
    # folder. Note that the path of the Dockerfile and context is relative to the *primary*
    # docker-compose.yml file (the first in the devcontainer.json "dockerComposeFile"
    # array). The sample below assumes your primary file is in the root of your project.
    #
    # build:
    #   context: .
    #   dockerfile: .devcontainer/Dockerfile

    # volumes:
    #   # Update this to wherever you want VS Code to mount the folder of your project
    #   - .:/workspace:cached

      # Uncomment the next line to use Docker from inside the container. See https://aka.ms/vscode-remote/samples/docker-from-docker-compose for details.
      # - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock

    # Uncomment the next four lines if you will use a ptrace-based debugger like C++, Go, and Rust.
    # cap_add:
    #   - SYS_PTRACE
    # security_opt:
    #   - seccomp:unconfined

    # Overrides default command so things don't shut down after the process ends.
    command: /bin/sh -c "while sleep 1000; do :; done"

```


```yml back/docker-compose.yml
version: "3"

networks:
  local:
    external: false

x-base-service: &base-service
  image: clickhouse/clickhouse-server:${CLICKHOUSE_VERSION:-23.6.2.18}
  restart: always
  ulimits:
    nofile:
      soft: 262144
      hard: 262144
  healthcheck:
    test: [ "CMD", "clickhouse-client", "-q", "SELECT 1" ]
    interval: 30s
    timeout: 5s
    retries: 3

services:
  rabbit:
    hostname: rabbit
    image: rabbitmq:latest
    environment:
      - RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_USER=admin
      - RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_PASS=mypass
    networks:
      - local
    ports:
      - "5672:5672"

  maildev:
    image: djfarrelly/maildev
    command: [ "bin/maildev", "--web", "80", "--smtp", "25" ]
    ports:
      - 8025:80
    networks:
      - local

  api:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: build/local/Dockerfile
    volumes:
      - .:/app/
      # - /home/irwin/work/egreen/ftp:/ftp
    depends_on:
      - db-0
      - rabbit
      - keydb
    # command: ./manage.py runserver 0.0.0.0:8000
    command: bash
    tty: true
    entrypoint: /entrypoint.sh
    environment:
      RABBIT_USER: admin
      RABBIT_PASSWORD: mypass
      RABBIT_HOST: rabbit
      RABBIT_PORT: 5672
      REDIS_CELERY_HOST: keydb
      REDIS_CELERY_PORT: 6379
      REDIS_CELERY_KEY_PASS: "passwd"
      REDIS_CELERY_USER: "default"
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: 'egreen'
      AMQP_HOST: 'rabbit'
      AMQP_ADMIN_HOST: 'rabbit'
    ports:
      - 8000:8000
    networks:
      - local

  celery-ui:

    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: build/local/Dockerfile
    volumes:
      - .:/app/
    depends_on:
      - db-0
      - rabbit
      - celery-worker
      - keydb
    entrypoint: /entrypoint.sh
    command: 'celery -A egreen flower --address=0.0.0.0 --port=5555 '
    ports:
      - 5555:5555
    environment:
      RABBIT_USER: admin
      RABBIT_PASSWORD: mypass
      RABBIT_HOST: rabbit
      RABBIT_PORT: 5672
      REDIS_CELERY_HOST: keydb
      REDIS_CELERY_PORT: 6379
      REDIS_CELERY_KEY_PASS: "passwd"
      REDIS_CELERY_USER: "default"
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: 'egreen'
    networks:
      - local

  celery-worker:

    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: build/local/Dockerfile
    volumes:
      - .:/app/
    depends_on:
      - db-0
      - rabbit
      - keydb
    entrypoint: /entrypoint.sh
    command: watchmedo auto-restart --directory=./ --pattern=*.py --recursive -- celery -A egreen worker --concurrency=20 --loglevel=info
    tty: true
    environment:
      RABBIT_USER: admin
      RABBIT_PASSWORD: mypass
      RABBIT_HOST: rabbit
      RABBIT_PORT: 5672
      REDIS_CELERY_HOST: keydb
      REDIS_CELERY_PORT: 6379
      REDIS_CELERY_KEY_PASS: "passwd"
      REDIS_CELERY_USER: "default"
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: 'egreen'
      AMQP_HOST: 'rabbit'
      AMQP_ADMIN_HOST: 'rabbit'
    networks:
      - local

  celery-beat:

    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: build/local/Dockerfile
    volumes:
      - ./:/app/
    depends_on:
      - db-0
      - rabbit
      - celery-worker
      - keydb
    entrypoint: /entrypoint.sh
    command: watchmedo auto-restart --directory=./ --pattern=*.py --recursive -- celery -A egreen beat --loglevel=info --scheduler django_celery_beat.schedulers:DatabaseScheduler
    tty: true
    environment:
      RABBIT_USER: admin
      RABBIT_PASSWORD: mypass
      RABBIT_HOST: rabbit
      RABBIT_PORT: 5672
      REDIS_CELERY_HOST: keydb
      REDIS_CELERY_PORT: 6379
      REDIS_CELERY_KEY_PASS: "passwd"
      REDIS_CELERY_USER: "default"
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: 'egreen'
      AMQP_HOST: 'rabbit'
      AMQP_ADMIN_HOST: 'rabbit'
    networks:
      - local

  keydb:
    image: eqalpha/keydb:x86_64_v6.3.2
    command: '/bin/sh -c "keydb-server --requirepass $$KEY_PASS"'
    environment:
      KEY_PASS: "passwd"
      KEY_USER: "default"
    volumes:
      - ./keydb.conf:/etc/keydb/keydb.conf
      - .:/app/
    ports:
      - 6379:6379
    networks:
      - local

  cache:
    image: eqalpha/keydb:x86_64_v6.3.2
    environment:
      KEY_PASS: "passwd"
      KEY_USER: "default"
    volumes:
      - ./keydb.conf:/etc/keydb/keydb.conf
      - .:/app/
    networks:
      - local

  db-0:
    image: docker.io/bitnami/postgresql-repmgr:14
    ports:
      - 5432
    volumes:
      - ./vc/dump:/dump
      - pg_0_data:/bitnami/postgresql@14
    environment:
      - POSTGRESQL_POSTGRES_PASSWORD=adminpassword
      - POSTGRESQL_USERNAME=egreen
      - POSTGRESQL_PASSWORD=egreen
      - POSTGRESQL_DATABASE=egreen
      - REPMGR_PASSWORD=repmgrpassword
      - REPMGR_PRIMARY_HOST=db-0
      - REPMGR_PRIMARY_PORT=5432
      - REPMGR_PARTNER_NODES=db-0,db-1:5432
      - REPMGR_NODE_NAME=db-0
      - REPMGR_NODE_NETWORK_NAME=db-0
      - REPMGR_PORT_NUMBER=5432

    networks:
      - local

  db-1:
    image: docker.io/bitnami/postgresql-repmgr:14
    ports:
      - 5432
    volumes:
      - pg_1_data:/bitnami/postgresql@14
    environment:
      - POSTGRESQL_POSTGRES_PASSWORD=adminpassword
      - POSTGRESQL_USERNAME=egreen
      - POSTGRESQL_PASSWORD=egreen
      - POSTGRESQL_DATABASE=egreen
      - REPMGR_PASSWORD=repmgrpassword
      - REPMGR_PRIMARY_HOST=db-0
      - REPMGR_PRIMARY_PORT=5432
      - REPMGR_PARTNER_NODES=db-0,db-1:5432
      - REPMGR_NODE_NAME=db-1
      - REPMGR_NODE_NETWORK_NAME=db-1
      - REPMGR_PORT_NUMBER=5432
    networks:
      - local

  # logs_db:
  #   image: clickhouse/clickhouse-server:24.1.5.6-alpine
  #   ports:
  #     - 8123:8123
  #   restart: always
  #   volumes:
  #     - log-data:/var/lib/clickhouse
  #   ulimits:
  #     nofile:
  #       soft: 262144
  #       hard: 262144
  #   networks:
  #     - local

  node1:
    <<: *base-service
    container_name: clickhouse-node1
    volumes:
      - "node1:/var/lib/clickhouse/"
      - "./vc/clickhouse/node1/:/etc/clickhouse-server/config.d/"
    ports:
      - "127.0.0.1:9000:9000"
    networks:
      - local
  node2:
    <<: *base-service
    container_name: clickhouse-node2
    volumes:
      - "node2:/var/lib/clickhouse/"
      - "./vc/clickhouse/node2/:/etc/clickhouse-server/config.d/"
    ports:
      - "127.0.0.1:9001:9000"
    networks:
      - local
  node3:
    <<: *base-service
    container_name: clickhouse-node3
    volumes:
      - "node3:/var/lib/clickhouse/"
      - "./vc/clickhouse/node3/:/etc/clickhouse-server/config.d/"
    ports:
      - "127.0.0.1:9002:9000"
    networks:
      - local
  node4:
    <<: *base-service
    container_name: clickhouse-node4
    volumes:
      - "node4:/var/lib/clickhouse/"
      - "./vc/clickhouse/node4/:/etc/clickhouse-server/config.d/"
    ports:
      - "127.0.0.1:9003:9000"
    networks:
      - local

  # sdata:
  #   hostname: sdata
  #   image: r.lab.egreen.fr/sensors/sdata:2.1.2
  #   environment:
  #     MONGO_PWD: eGreen1
  #     MONGO_USER: egreen
  #     MONGO_URL: db-mongo
  #   networks:
  #     - local
  #   ports:
  #     - "8080:8080"

  # db-mongo:
  #   image: mongo:3.6
  #   ports:
  #     - 27017:27017
  #   volumes:
  #     - ./vc/api/mongo-datadb:/data/db
  #     - ./vc/dump-mongo:/data/dump
  #   environment:
  #     MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_USERNAME: egreen
  #     MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_PASSWORD: eGreen1
  #   networks:
  #     - local
  #   deploy:
  #     resources:
  #       limits:
  #         cpus: "2"      # CPU limit, specify the number of CPU cores
  #         memory: "1G"

  ftp:
    build:
      context: ./build/local/ftp
    environment:
      ADDRESS: ftp
      USERS: egreen|passwdpasswd
    volumes:
      - ./ftp:/ftp/egreen
    networks:
      - local

volumes:
  pg_0_data:
    driver: local
  pg_1_data:
    driver: local
  node1:
    name: clickhouse-node1
  node2:
    name: clickhouse-node2
  node3:
    name: clickhouse-node3
  node4:
    name: clickhouse-node4
```


How to configure zed to use devcontainer and run lsp inside it ?
I'll help you configure Zed to use the devcontainer. You'll need to create a `.zed` directory with the proper configuration.

<patch>
<title>Add Zed devcontainer config</title>

<edit>
<path>back/.zed/settings.json</path>
<operation>create</operation>
<new_text>{
  "dev_container": {
    "enabled": true,
    "compose_file": [
      "docker-compose.yml",
      ".devcontainer/docker-compose.yml"
    ],
    "service": "api"
  },
  "language_servers": {
    "python": {
      "enabled": true,
      "command": ["pylsp"],
      "environment": {
        "PYTHONPATH": "/app"
      }
    }
  },
  "formatter": {
    "python": {
      "command": "black",
      "arguments": ["--quiet", "-"]
    }
  }
}
</new_text>
</edit>

<edit>
<path>back/.devcontainer/devcontainer.json</path>
<description>Add python-lsp-server to VS Code extensions</description>
<operation>update</operation>
<old_text>	"extensions": [
		"ms-python.python",
		"njpwerner.autodocstring",
		"ms-python.black-formatter",
		"GitHub.copilot"
	]</old_text>
<new_text>	"extensions": [
		"ms-python.python",
		"njpwerner.autodocstring",
		"ms-python.black-formatter",
		"GitHub.copilot",
		"python-lsp-server"
	],
	"postCreateCommand": "pip install 'python-lsp-server[all]' black pylint"</new_text>
</edit>

</patch>

This configuration will:

1. Enable devcontainer support in Zed
2. Use the same docker-compose files as VS Code
3. Configure the Python LSP server to run inside the container
4. Set up Black as the Python formatter
5. Install necessary Python packages (python-lsp-server, black, pylint) when the container is created

To use this:

1. Create the `.zed` directory in your `back` folder 
2. Add these settings
3. Restart Zed
4. Open the project - Zed should automatically detect and use the devcontainer

The LSP server will run inside the container, giving you proper code intelligence while keeping all dependencies isolated in the container environment.

Let me know if you need any clarification or run into issues!
